next_steps: Perform lumbar puncture with measurement of opening pressure and analysis of fluid for cell count, glucose, protein, gram stain, and fungal culture
Obtain MRI of the brain with magnetic resonance venography (MRV)
Consult neurology for formal evaluation of new-onset headache in a patient with recent procedural history
Review coagulation profile and platelet function given the cirrhotic state and history of recent procedures

differential_diagnoses: Spontaneous bacterial peritonitis with secondary reactive meningitis
next_steps: Urgent MRI brain with MR venography (MRV) to rule out venous thrombosis and structural intracranial lesions
Evaluation of coagulation profile (INR, PTT, platelet count) and fibrinogen
Ophthalmologic examination for papilledema or signs of raised intracranial pressure
Lumbar puncture for opening pressure, cell count, protein, glucose, Gram stain, fungal culture, and cryptococcal antigen, pending correction of significant coagulopathy
Assessment of serum electrolytes (sodium) and ammonia to exclude metabolic triggers for headache
